annotations.label: Transcendental number

anchor.snippet: a transcendental number is a real or complex number that is not algebraic

annotations.note: `Transcendental R x` is defined as `¬IsAlgebraic R x`, matching 'not algebraic' (over ℚ/ℤ for real/complex numbers).

annotations.label: Almost all numbers are transcendental

anchor.snippet: almost all real and complex numbers are transcendental

annotations.note: Algebraic numbers are countable (so their complement is co-countable/full-measure), but no decl states 'almost all numbers are transcendental' directly.

annotations.label: Real transcendentals are irrational

anchor.snippet: All transcendental real numbers (also known as real transcendental numbers or transcendental irrational numbers ) are irrational

annotations.note: `Transcendental.irrational` proves `Transcendental ℚ r → Irrational r`.

annotations.label: Square root of 2 is not transcendental

anchor.snippet: the square root of 2 is an irrational number, but it is not a transcendental number

annotations.note: `irrational_sqrt_two` gives √2 irrational; its algebraicity (root of X²−2) follows trivially from `IsAlgebraic` but is not a stated decl.

annotations.label: sin x is not an algebraic function

anchor.snippet: sin x is not an algebraic function of x

annotations.note: Transcendence of the sine function as an algebraic function is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lambert's conjecture on e and π

anchor.snippet: conjectured that e and π were both transcendental numbers

annotations.note: Neither the transcendence of e nor of π is proven in Mathlib (only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass).

annotations.label: Existence of transcendental numbers (Liouville)

anchor.snippet: Joseph Liouville first proved the existence of transcendental numbers

annotations.note: `transcendental_liouvilleNumber` exhibits an explicit transcendental real number, witnessing existence.

annotations.label: Liouville numbers

anchor.snippet: this class of numbers is called the Liouville numbers

annotations.note: `Liouville x` is defined by the standard rational-approximation property.

annotations.label: All Liouville numbers are transcendental

anchor.snippet: Liouville showed that all Liouville numbers are transcendental

annotations.note: `Liouville.transcendental` proves `Liouville x → Transcendental ℤ x`.

annotations.label: e is transcendental (Hermite)

anchor.snippet: The first number to be proven transcendental without having been specifically constructed

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass is formalized; the transcendence of e itself is not yet proven.

annotations.label: Cantor: algebraic countable, reals uncountable

anchor.snippet: the algebraic numbers are countable and the real numbers are uncountable

annotations.note: `Algebraic.countable` gives countability of algebraic numbers and `Cardinal.not_countable_real` gives uncountability of ℝ.

annotations.label: π is transcendental (Lindemann)

anchor.snippet: Ferdinand von Lindemann published the first complete proof that π is transcendental

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass exists; π's transcendence is not proven, though `irrational_pi` proves the weaker irrationality.

annotations.label: e^a transcendental for nonzero algebraic a

anchor.snippet: He first proved that e a is transcendental if a is a non-zero algebraic number

annotations.note: This is Lindemann–Weierstrass; only its analytical part is formalized, not the transcendence conclusion.

annotations.label: Euler's identity

anchor.snippet: e iπ = −1 is algebraic (see Euler's identity )

annotations.note: `Complex.exp_pi_mul_I` states `exp (π * I) = -1`, the core identity used here.

annotations.label: Squaring the circle impossible

anchor.snippet: squaring the circle

annotations.note: The impossibility of squaring the circle (a consequence of π's transcendence) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gelfond–Schneider theorem

anchor.snippet: The affirmative answer was provided in 1934 by the Gelfond–Schneider theorem

annotations.note: The Gelfond–Schneider theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Baker's theorem on linear forms in logarithms

anchor.snippet: his work on lower bounds for linear forms in any number of logarithms

annotations.note: Baker's theorem on linear forms in logarithms of algebraic numbers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Transcendental number (not root of integer polynomial)

anchor.snippet: A transcendental number is a (possibly complex) number that is not the root of any integer polynomial

annotations.note: `Transcendental ℤ x` is exactly 'not a root of any nonzero integer polynomial', via `transcendental_iff`.

annotations.label: Real transcendental implies irrational

anchor.snippet: Every real transcendental number must also be irrational

annotations.note: `Transcendental.irrational` proves `Transcendental ℚ r → Irrational r`.

annotations.label: Transcendental numbers are uncountable

anchor.snippet: The set of transcendental numbers is uncountably infinite

annotations.note: Follows from `Algebraic.countable` plus `Cardinal.not_countable_real`, but uncountability of the transcendentals is not a single stated decl.

annotations.label: Cantor's diagonal argument

anchor.snippet: Cantor's diagonal argument proves that the real numbers (and therefore also the complex numbers ) are uncountable

annotations.note: Uncountability of ℝ (and ℂ) is in Mathlib, established via diagonalization-style cardinality arguments.

annotations.label: Irrationals contain real transcendentals and some algebraics

anchor.snippet: The irrational numbers contain all the real transcendental numbers and a subset of the algebraic numbers

annotations.note: `Transcendental.irrational` gives real transcendentals ⊆ irrationals, but the full set-containment statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Algebraic function of transcendental is transcendental

anchor.snippet: Applying any non-constant single-variable algebraic function to a transcendental argument yields a transcendental value

annotations.note: `Transcendental.aeval` covers non-constant polynomials applied to a transcendental, a special case of general algebraic functions.

annotations.label: At least one of a+b and ab is transcendental

anchor.snippet: at least one of a + b and ab must be transcendental

annotations.note: No decl states that at least one of the sum and product of two transcendentals is transcendental.

annotations.label: π and 1−π sum to an algebraic number

anchor.snippet: π and (1 − π ) are both transcendental, but

annotations.note: This example depends on the unformalized transcendence of π and is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers form an algebraically closed field

anchor.snippet: algebraic numbers form an algebraically closed field

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the algebraic closure of ℚ in ℂ (i.e., the algebraic numbers) is algebraically closed; the precise idiomatic statement is via `IsAlgClosure`/`AlgebraicClosure`.

annotations.label: Non-computable numbers are strict subset of transcendentals

anchor.snippet: The non-computable numbers are a strict subset of the transcendental numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of computable real numbers tied to transcendence.

annotations.label: Liouville numbers transcendental but not conversely

anchor.snippet: All Liouville numbers are transcendental, but not vice versa

annotations.note: `Liouville.transcendental` proves the forward direction; the converse-failure (a transcendental that is not Liouville) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Transcendentals with bounded partial quotients exist

anchor.snippet: there exist transcendental numbers which have bounded partial quotients

annotations.note: No result on transcendence of continued fractions with bounded partial quotients is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: e is not a Liouville number

anchor.snippet: one can show that e is not a Liouville number

annotations.note: Mathlib does not prove that e is not a Liouville number.

annotations.label: π is not a Liouville number (Mahler)

anchor.snippet: Kurt Mahler showed in 1953 that π is also not a Liouville number

annotations.note: Mahler's result that π is not a Liouville number is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conjecture on bounded continued fractions

anchor.snippet: It is conjectured that all infinite continued fractions with bounded terms

annotations.note: This open conjecture about continued fractions is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: π is transcendental

anchor.snippet: Numbers proven to be transcendental

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass is formalized; π's transcendence is not (though `irrational_pi` proves irrationality).

annotations.label: e^a transcendental; Euler's number e

anchor.snippet: in particular Euler's number e

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass exists; transcendence of e and e^a is not proven.

annotations.label: Gelfond's constant

anchor.snippet: where n is a positive integer; in particular Gelfond's constant

annotations.note: Transcendence of Gelfond's constant e^π (Gelfond–Schneider) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Algebraic combinations of π and e^π

anchor.snippet: following from their algebraic independence

annotations.note: Algebraic independence of π and e^π is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: a^b and Gelfond–Schneider constant

anchor.snippet: where a is algebraic but not 0 or 1, and b is irrational algebraic

annotations.note: The Gelfond–Schneider theorem and its constant 2^√2 are not formalized.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm of algebraic numbers

anchor.snippet: The natural logarithm ln( a ) if a is algebraic and not equal to 0 or 1

annotations.note: Transcendence of ln(a) for algebraic a (Lindemann–Weierstrass) is not formalized.

annotations.label: log_a b transcendental (Gelfond–Schneider)

anchor.snippet: if a and b are positive integers not both powers of the same integer

annotations.note: Transcendence of such logarithms (Gelfond–Schneider) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sums of logarithms (Baker's theorem)

anchor.snippet: by Baker's theorem

annotations.note: Baker's theorem on linear forms in logarithms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Trigonometric functions at algebraic arguments

anchor.snippet: The trigonometric functions sin( x ), cos( x ) and their hyperbolic counterparts

annotations.note: Transcendence of trig values at nonzero algebraic arguments is not formalized.

annotations.label: Inverse trigonometric functions at algebraic arguments

anchor.snippet: Non-zero results of the inverse trigonometric functions

annotations.note: Transcendence of inverse trig values at algebraic arguments is not formalized.

annotations.label: Transcendence for rational x

anchor.snippet: for rational x such that

annotations.note: No such transcendence result for special-function values at rational x is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dottie number

anchor.snippet: The Dottie number d (the fixed point of the cosine function)

annotations.note: Transcendence of the Dottie number is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lambert W function values; omega constant

anchor.snippet: for any branch of the Lambert W function

annotations.note: Transcendence of Lambert W values and the omega constant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generalized Lambert W function values

anchor.snippet: for any branch of the generalized Lambert W function

annotations.note: Transcendence of generalized Lambert W values is not formalized.

annotations.label: Square super-root of natural numbers

anchor.snippet: the square super-root of any natural number is either an integer or transcendental

annotations.note: This dichotomy for the square super-root is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gamma function at certain rationals

anchor.snippet: Values of the gamma function of rational numbers

annotations.note: Transcendence of Γ at rational arguments is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lemniscate constant

anchor.snippet: such as the lemniscate constant

annotations.note: Transcendence of the lemniscate constant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Beta function at non-integer rationals

anchor.snippet: The values of Beta function

annotations.note: Transcendence of Beta function values is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bessel function values and quotients

anchor.snippet: The Bessel function of the first kind

annotations.note: Transcendence results for Bessel function values are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bessel-function combination with Euler–Mascheroni constant

anchor.snippet: are Bessel functions and γ is the Euler–Mascheroni constant

annotations.note: This Bessel/γ combination transcendence result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fibonacci zeta at even arguments

anchor.snippet: Values of the Fibonacci zeta function at positive even arguments

annotations.note: Transcendence of Fibonacci zeta values is not formalized.

annotations.label: Liouville's constant

anchor.snippet: Any Liouville number , in particular: Liouville's constant

annotations.note: The `liouvilleNumber` family (including the classical base constant) is proven transcendental by `transcendental_liouvilleNumber`.

annotations.label: Numbers with irrationality measure > 2 (Roth)

anchor.snippet: Numbers with irrationality measure larger than 2

annotations.note: The irrationality-measure predicate `LiouvilleWith` is defined, but the Thue–Siegel–Roth transcendence consequence is not proven.

annotations.label: Numbers constructed to not be periods

anchor.snippet: Numbers artificially constructed not to be algebraic periods

annotations.note: The theory of periods is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chaitin's constant

anchor.snippet: Any non-computable number , in particular: Chaitin's constant

annotations.note: Chaitin's constant and non-computable reals are not connected to transcendence in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Numbers not simply normal in any base

anchor.snippet: Constructed irrational numbers which are not simply normal in any base

annotations.note: Normality of numbers and this transcendence criterion are not formalized.

annotations.label: Numbers with Sturmian-word digits

anchor.snippet: form a Sturmian word

annotations.note: Transcendence of numbers with Sturmian digit sequences is not formalized.

annotations.label: Prouhet–Thue–Morse and rabbit constants

anchor.snippet: The Prouhet–Thue–Morse constant

annotations.note: Transcendence of the Thue–Morse and rabbit constants is not formalized.

annotations.label: Komornik–Loreti constant

anchor.snippet: The Komornik–Loreti constant

annotations.note: Transcendence of the Komornik–Loreti constant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Paperfolding constant

anchor.snippet: The paperfolding constant

annotations.note: Transcendence of the paperfolding constant is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fast-convergence series (Gao)

anchor.snippet: The values of the infinite series with fast convergence rate

annotations.note: This fast-convergence-series transcendence result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Numbers built from polynomial ratios

anchor.snippet: are polynomials in variables

annotations.note: This construction's transcendence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Numbers defined via the floor function

anchor.snippet: is the floor function

annotations.note: Transcendence of these floor-function constructions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Moser–de Bruijn digit numbers

anchor.snippet: whose nonzero digit positions are given by the Moser–de Bruijn sequence

annotations.note: Transcendence of Moser–de Bruijn digit numbers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rogers–Ramanujan continued fraction values

anchor.snippet: The values of the Rogers-Ramanujan continued fraction

annotations.note: Transcendence of Rogers–Ramanujan continued fraction values is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lemniscatic theta-function values

anchor.snippet: The lemniscatic values of theta function

annotations.note: Transcendence of lemniscatic theta-function values is not formalized.

annotations.label: j-invariant at non-imaginary-quadratic algebraics

anchor.snippet: is algebraic but not imaginary quadratic

annotations.note: Transcendence of j-invariant values at such arguments is not formalized.

annotations.label: Constants in Gijswijt's sequence formula

anchor.snippet: in the formula for first index of occurrence of Gijswijt's sequence

annotations.note: These constants in Gijswijt's sequence formula are not formalized.

annotations.label: Combinations of transcendentals not known transcendental

anchor.snippet: Most nontrivial combinations of two or more transcendental numbers

annotations.note: This meta-observation about open problems is not formalized.

annotations.label: e+π and π/e satisfy no low-degree polynomial

anchor.snippet: do not satisfy any polynomial equation of degree ≤ 8

annotations.note: These bounds on e+π and π/e are not formalized.

annotations.label: At least one of e^e and e^{e^2} transcendental

anchor.snippet: At least one of the numbers e e and e e 2 is transcendental

annotations.note: This 'at least one' transcendence result is not formalized.

annotations.label: At least one of eπ and e+π transcendental

anchor.snippet: at least one of the numbers eπ and e + π is transcendental

annotations.note: This classic 'at least one of eπ, e+π' result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Schanuel's conjecture implication

anchor.snippet: Schanuel's conjecture would imply that all of the above numbers are transcendental and algebraically independent

annotations.note: Schanuel's conjecture is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler–Lehmer constants: at most one algebraic

anchor.snippet: an infinite list of Euler-Lehmer constants (which includes γ /4 ) contains at most one algebraic number

annotations.note: This result on Euler–Lehmer constants is not formalized.

annotations.label: At least one of γ and Gompertz constant transcendental

anchor.snippet: at least one of γ and the Gompertz constant δ is transcendental

annotations.note: This 'at least one of γ, Gompertz' result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann zeta at odd integers; Apéry's constant

anchor.snippet: in particular Apéry's constant ζ (3) , which is known to be irrational

annotations.note: Neither irrationality of ζ(3) (Apéry) nor transcendence of ζ at odd integers is formalized.

annotations.label: At least one of ζ(4k+3) terms transcendental

anchor.snippet: at least one of the numbers ζ (4 k +3) and

annotations.note: This conditional transcendence statement about ζ values is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dirichlet beta at even integers; Catalan's constant

anchor.snippet: in particular Catalan's Constant β (2)

annotations.note: Transcendence/irrationality of Catalan's constant and Dirichlet beta values is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gamma values Γ(1/n) unknown irrationality

anchor.snippet: Values of the Gamma Function Γ (1/ n ) for positive integers

annotations.note: Results on Γ(1/n) values are not formalized.

annotations.label: At least one of Γ(1/n), Γ(2/n) transcendental

anchor.snippet: at least one the numbers Γ(1/ n ) and Γ(2/ n ) is transcendental

annotations.note: This 'at least one of Γ(1/n), Γ(2/n)' result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-obviously-algebraic limits

anchor.snippet: Any number given by some kind of limit that is not obviously algebraic

annotations.note: This heuristic meta-statement is not a formalizable theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: e is transcendental

anchor.section: A proof that e is transcendental
anchor.snippet: The first proof that the base of the natural logarithms, e , is transcendental dates from 1873

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of the Lindemann–Weierstrass machinery is formalized; e's transcendence is not yet concluded.

annotations.label: Lemma 1

anchor.snippet: There are arbitrarily large k such that

annotations.note: This proof-specific lemma corresponds to the analytical estimates in `exp_polynomial_approx`, but is not isolated as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lemma 2

anchor.snippet: For sufficiently large k

annotations.note: This proof-specific bound corresponds to the analytical machinery in `exp_polynomial_approx`, but is not isolated as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: π is transcendental

anchor.snippet: can be used to show that the number π is transcendental

annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass is formalized; π's transcendence is not concluded, though `irrational_pi` proves irrationality.
